reasoning: As of Jan 2025, 47 countries had ratified AfCFTA, explicitly omitting Libya. While reports in Feb/March 2025 show 48 countries have now ratified, there is zero indication from multiple specific news searches that Libya was the 48th country. Furthermore, with only two months remaining until the July 1, 2025 deadline and Libya still facing severe political fragmentation between rival administrations, rapid ratification of a continental trade agreement appears highly improbable. Base rates for such actions in politically unstable countries in short time windows are near zero. I forecast broadly at the minimum probability boundary due to the total lack of evidence of ongoing legislative efforts or plans in Libya to ratify the agreement.
